methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Natron Energy is a pre-exit frontier-tech company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised over $200M. The relevant comp universe consists of 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private peers, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 0 companies meet narrow stage and valuation-tier filters within Climate & Energy—making traditional peer-median methodology inapplicable.

No triangulated valuation range can be reliably established from the available data. The peer-median approach fails the prerequisite threshold of three or more comparable datapoints at matching stage and tier. While 21 same-sector peers exist broadly in Climate & Energy, none satisfy the filtering criteria necessary to produce a defensible median multiple. An all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of capital intensity, commercialization timelines, and technology risk profiles across energy subsectors.

Valuation assessment requires either (1) acquisition-based analysis if terms are disclosed, (2) DCF modeling anchored to management projections and technology milestones, or (3) expansion of the comp set to include cross-sector hardware and deep-tech peers with comparable burn rates and funding profiles. The key uncertainty remains whether Natron's capital intensity and exit multiple align more closely with battery-storage platforms or broader energy-infrastructure comparables. Further disclosure of acquisition terms or forward revenue guidance would be necessary to establish a credible valuation range.
